entities.name: W NATIONAL PARK
entities.description: A trans-boundary biosphere reserve in West Africa experiencing fragmentation and degradation

entities.name: WEST AFRICA
entities.description: The geographical region where the study was conducted

entities.name: SOIL SEED BANK
entities.description: The reservoir of dormant seeds stored in the soil layers

entities.name: ABOVE-GROUND VEGETATION
entities.description: The standing plant species present on the surface of the land

entities.name: VEGETATION SUCCESSION
entities.description: The process of change in the species structure of an ecological community over time

entities.name: DISTURBANCE GRADIENT
entities.description: The range of habitat disturbance severity categorized as low, intermediate, and high

entities.name: SEEDLING EMERGENCE TECHNIQUE
entities.description: A technique used to analyze the composition and size of the soil seed bank

entities.name: NON-WOODY TAXA
entities.description: Plant groups that are not woody and dominated the soil seed samples

entities.name: WOODY VEGETATION
entities.description: Plant groups that are woody and showed limited potential for restoration from soil seed banks

entities.name: VEGETATION RESTORATION
entities.description: The process of restoring degraded sites to their original vegetation state

entities.name: ANTHROPOGENIC IMPACTS
entities.description: Human-caused disturbances, such as intense fire, that affect the ecosystem

entities.name: SITE MANIPULATION
entities.description: The process of improving environmental conditions to facilitate seedling establishment and growth

entities.name: SOWING SEEDS
entities.description: The practice of planting seeds collected from other locations to restore forests

entities.name: PLANTING TREE SEEDLINGS
entities.description: The practice of planting young trees to restore forests

relationships.description: Influences the availability of propagules for succession
Has a relationship with the standing vegetation
Affects the density and composition of seeds in the soil
Used to analyze the soil seed bank
Provides potential but limited source for restoring woody vegetation
Dominates the samples across all soil depths and habitats
Relies less on soil seed banks for regeneration than on recently shed seeds
Must be alleviated to accelerate forest recovery
Used to improve conditions for seedling establishment and growth
Study site where disturbance gradient was assessed
